title: A Claymation Brand Film, Built in Masonry
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~48s. Featured brand: Liquid Death. Inspired by Tim Koda (DVmqeZlDD2u, a Bad Bunny DtMF claymation rebuild). Full editor brief: visual, Masonry generation, supers, edit, transitions, SFX.
concept: A build-it-in-Masonry stop-motion brand film for Liquid Death. The hook: this warm claymation clip, fingerprints in the clay and tiny wobbles between frames, was not shot in a studio, it was built in Masonry. It breaks down the three-step creative workflow: the character (generate the clay mascot model and material), the scene (build the environment and keep it consistent), and the animation (stop-motion frame by frame, keeping the imperfections that give it soul). The inspiration rebuilt Bad Bunny's DtMF clip with another tool; here the integration is Masonry and the demo brand is Liquid Death.
audio: Warm orchestral claymation score with a subtle metal edge (true to Liquid Death). Audio-only: no voiceover. Supers carry the workflow; cut to the score, with clay-squish and shutter SFX.

beats.super: Clay-textured 3D letters, warm, tumble in like stop-motion. 'Masonry' emphasized.
beats.visual: A cozy claymation diorama: a clay character in a warm lamplit room, hand-made texture everywhere. Hero clay tallboy can on the table.
beats.masonry: Generate the opening claymation frame in Masonry: prompt 'stop-motion clay diorama, character in a warm lamplit room, handmade clay texture, tallboy can on the table, 9:16.' This is the look the whole film is built from.
beats.edit: Slow stop-motion-style push-in with a faint frame-step stutter (drop to ~12fps feel).
beats.transition: Stop-motion stutter cut on the beat.
beats.sfx: Warm score opens; a soft clay-squish and a film-shutter tick.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 0-1: 'was made / DtMF' clay hook.

beats.ost: it could have been a real studio
beats.visual: Same diorama, tighter; you can see fingerprint dents and seams in the clay.
beats.masonry: Hold the Masonry frame and prompt 'visible fingerprints, clay seams' so the handmade detail reads. That detail is what makes it pass as a real studio build.
beats.edit: Slow macro drift across the clay surface.

beats.ost: the full workflow, end to end
beats.super: 'workflow' bold; 'end to end' resolves under it.
beats.visual: A clean title beat over the diorama, signalling the breakdown to come.
beats.masonry: Three steps ahead: character, scene, animation, all inside Masonry. No physical clay, no rig.
beats.edit: Title hold with a faint zoom.

beats.super: 'STEP 1' tag, then 'the character' in bold clay letters.
beats.visual: The clay mascot reveal: a characterful Liquid Death clay figure (the brand's irreverent spirit), full body, hero lighting.
beats.masonry: Step 1, the character: in Masonry prompt 'original clay mascot for Liquid Death, full body, stop-motion model, hero light.' Generate the model that will carry the whole film.
beats.edit: Slow reveal pan up the character, stop-motion cadence.
beats.sfx: Clay-squish accent as the character lands.

beats.ost: drop the model and the material
beats.super: 'model' and 'material' snap in as mono UI tags over a Masonry panel.
beats.visual: Masonry UI beside the character: the clay model selected, a clay material applied; the figure updates to matte clay.
beats.masonry: In Masonry set the model and the material: choose the character model, apply the clay material so it renders as true stop-motion clay, not CG plastic.
beats.edit: UI zoom on the model and material selectors, then back to the character.
beats.sfx: UI clicks on the model and material selects.

beats.visual: A short prompt typed into Masonry; the character restyles to the final clay look.
beats.masonry: Prompt the character's final look in one step: 'matte clay, Liquid Death colorway, expressive face.' Masonry locks the design.
beats.edit: Type-on of the prompt, then a quick restyle reveal.

beats.super: 'STEP 2' tag, then 'the scene' in clay letters.
beats.visual: The environment builds around the character: the warm room set, props, depth, the tallboy can staged on the table.
beats.masonry: Step 2, the scene: in Masonry prompt the environment around the locked character, 'cozy lamplit room, wooden table, Liquid Death tallboy prop.' Build the world.
beats.edit: Slow reveal pull-back from character to full set.

beats.ost: keep it the same every frame
beats.visual: The same set shown from two angles, identical props and lighting, the character consistent.
beats.masonry: Prompt the environment, then seed-lock it so every shot of the scene stays identical: same props, same light, same character. Consistency is the whole game.
beats.edit: Two angle cuts of the same set; match the character position.
beats.sfx: Subtle drone holding the scene steady.

beats.super: Single word, bold, centered, clay texture.
beats.visual: A grid or quick sweep of the character in the same set across several shots, all matching.
beats.masonry: Generate the coverage you need from the locked scene in Masonry: wide, medium, close, all consistent because the seed and brand kit are fixed.

beats.super: 'STEP 3' tag, then 'the animation' in clay letters.
beats.visual: The character begins to move: a small gesture, frame-stepped, classic stop-motion cadence.
beats.masonry: Step 3, the animation: in Masonry animate the locked character with a stop-motion frame cadence, 'frame by frame, 12fps clay motion.'
beats.edit: Show the motion at a deliberate stop-motion frame rate.

beats.super: Three short tags snap in on their own sub-cuts, mono.
beats.visual: Masonry UI driving the animation: the model, a stop-motion toggle, a motion prompt; the character performs.
beats.masonry: In Masonry: pick the model, enable the stop-motion look, and prompt the motion. The character acts out the beat without a physical rig.
beats.edit: Three quick UI sub-cuts, then the character performing.
beats.sfx: UI clicks; a clay-squish on the move.

beats.super: No super, let the animation play.
beats.visual: The full stop-motion shot plays: the clay character moving in the cozy set, reaching for the Liquid Death tallboy, warm and characterful.
beats.masonry: Render the hero animated shot in Masonry from the locked character and scene. This is the payoff: a finished claymation beat, no studio.
beats.edit: Hold the performance; let it breathe at stop-motion cadence.
beats.sfx: Score swells; warm wood and clay foley.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 30-34: the animation playing out.

beats.super: 'imperfection' bold; 'the same tiny wobbles' resolves under it.
beats.visual: Macro on the clay: fingerprints, a tiny wobble between frames, a seam. The soul of claymation.
beats.masonry: In Masonry keep the imperfections: the slight frame-to-frame wobble, fingerprints, uneven edges. That is what separates real-feeling claymation from sterile CG.
beats.edit: Extreme clay macro with a deliberate frame jitter.
beats.sfx: Intimate clay-creak; the metal edge of the score peeks through.

beats.super: 'All' bold, then a clean hero lockup.
beats.visual: Hero wide: the clay character with the Liquid Death tallboy in the finished, warmly lit diorama. A complete brand film frame.
beats.masonry: Composite the final shot in Masonry: character, scene and animation together, with the Liquid Death tallboy as the hero prop. One coherent claymation world.
beats.edit: Slow hero push-in on the finished frame.
beats.sfx: Full score; a satisfying can-crack accent (true to Liquid Death).
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 39-43: hero shots coming together.

beats.ost: Comment CLAY for the full workflow
beats.super: 'CLAY' in clay letters, then the Liquid Death plus Masonry end card.
beats.visual: Final beat: the character and tallboy, then the Liquid Death plus Masonry end card.
beats.masonry: Close on the hero Masonry frame, then the Liquid Death plus Masonry end card. Three tools, three steps, one claymation brand film, all in Masonry.
beats.edit: Settle on the character, clean cut to the end card.
beats.transition: Final stutter cut to the end card; hold.
beats.sfx: Score resolves; a final clay-squish and shutter to button it.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 44-47: 'Comment / full / workflow' CTA close.

notes: Editor workflow: (1) In Masonry generate the clay mascot character (model + clay material), then build and seed-lock the cozy diorama scene with a Liquid Death tallboy prop. (2) Animate the locked character with a stop-motion frame cadence; keep the imperfections (fingerprints, frame wobble). (3) Edit per beat following the Super style, Edit / Motion, Transition and SFX direction; audio-only, no voiceover, run the whole thing at a stop-motion frame feel. The inspiration rebuilt Bad Bunny's DtMF clip with another tool; the integration here is Masonry. Liquid Death is a stand-in demo brand: swap in the real client. No media generated yet, generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
refsummary: The original (Tim Koda) is a claymation breakdown, a stop-motion rebuild of Bad Bunny's DtMF clip made with an AI tool, walking the character, the scene and the animation step by step with the clay imperfections kept in. Watch it for the warm stop-motion texture and the three-step workflow; our version makes the integration Masonry.
story: Same three-step stop-motion breakdown, reframed to Masonry with Liquid Death as the demo brand. Arc: HOOK, this warm claymation, fingerprints and frame wobble and all, was built in Masonry, not a studio. STEP 1 the character, generate the clay mascot model and material. STEP 2 the scene, build the environment and keep it consistent every frame. STEP 3 the animation, stop-motion frame by frame, keeping the imperfections that give it soul. CLOSE, it all comes together, comment CLAY. The viewer should believe a full stop-motion brand film is buildable in Masonry.
takeaway: A full claymation, stop-motion brand film, imperfections and soul included, can be built end to end in Masonry without a studio or a single physical set.
